sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: My sister has always dreamed of becoming a professional (1) ...... (ENTERTAIN), so she signed up for a performing arts course as soon as she left school.

After a lengthy (2) ...... (CONVERSE) with her drama tutor about the (3) ...... (BORE) of doing the same routines night after night, she decided to write her own material and start booking solo (4) ...... (PERFORM) at comedy clubs around the city.
She's doing brilliantly! She is (5) ...... (CURRENT) rehearsing a brand new show that she hopes to take on a national tour.
There was genuine (6) ...... (EXCITE) in the theatre when the director announced the names of the lead cast members.

A great stand-up comedian needs real (7) ...... (VARY) in their set, mixing stories the crowd finds (8) ...... (AMUSE) with sharp impressions of (9) ...... (FAME) politicians, and always keeping the audience (10) ...... (ACT) engaged until the very last punchline.

sections.title: B: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
We didn't throw an after-show party for the cast; we took them out for dinner instead. **instead**
We took the cast out for dinner ...... an after-show party.

It doesn't surprise me that Sandra missed every single rehearsal. **just**
It ...... miss every single rehearsal.

I'm sure the fans will go wild when the headline act walks on stage. **bound**
The fans ...... wild when the headline act walks on stage.

You shouldn't record videos during a live concert. **supposed**
You ...... videos during a live concert.

At least she said she was sorry that she'd ruined the film's ending! **apologised**
At least she ...... the film's ending!

The producers don't mind you attending the premiere with the cast. **happy**
The producers ...... attend the premiere with the cast.

Don't tease Marcus about his stage fright as he doesn't like it. **fun**
Don't ...... Marcus about his stage fright as he doesn't like it.

That live concert really affected me. **impression**
That live concert really ...... me.

sections.title: C: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold
sections.instructions: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold.

I felt so **disappointed** when the lead singer cancelled the show just two hours before it was due to start.
Despite touring together for years, the two band members apparently don't **have a good relationship** with each other offstage.
The drama club is going to **perform** a musical at the end of the school year.
Danny **resembles** his grandfather; they both share a passion for classic jazz music.
The presenter told the audience that the show had been cancelled and everyone **believed** it!
Buster Keaton will always **be remembered** as one of the greatest comic talents in the history of silent film.
You can always **trust** the orchestra to deliver a stunning performance at the annual gala.
...... three consecutive Grammy Awards, she is now considered one of the most influential voices of her generation.

The comedian, ...... I last saw perform at a tiny club several years ago, now sells out arenas worldwide.
And the track ...... by our listeners as the best new release this month is Midnight Rain by The Silver Keys.
'You didn't get the role in the film, did you?' 'No, and that's the reason ...... I decided to focus on theatre instead!'
Could you ...... in the listings magazine and see which shows are on this weekend?
She's writing a ...... set in 1950s New Orleans, following the life of a struggling jazz musician.
The comedian kept the whole crowd ...... from the moment he walked on stage to his final bow.

I wasn't sure about this film's soundtrack at first, but it's really starting to ...... on me now.
